context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements on-demand ISR revalidation with Authorization header security, targeted use of revalidatePath and revalidateTag, correct event type handling, and semantic fetch cache tags in the data layer.

checklist.name: Authorization header check
checklist.description: app/api/revalidate/route.ts reads the 'authorization' header and returns a 401 response if it does not match 'Bearer <token>' (using an env variable for the token)

checklist.name: revalidatePath used
checklist.description: app/api/revalidate/route.ts calls revalidatePath() for specific page paths (e.g. /products/${handle})

checklist.name: revalidateTag used
checklist.description: app/api/revalidate/route.ts calls revalidateTag() to invalidate named cache tags

checklist.name: product event type handled
checklist.description: app/api/revalidate/route.ts handles a 'product' event type by revalidating the specific product path and/or 'products' tag

checklist.name: collection event type handled
checklist.description: app/api/revalidate/route.ts handles a 'collection' event type by revalidating the specific collection path and/or 'collections' tag

checklist.name: all event type handled
checklist.description: app/api/revalidate/route.ts handles an 'all' event type that revalidates both 'products' and 'collections' tags

checklist.name: Semantic tag names in data layer
checklist.description: lib/get-product.ts assigns fetch cache tags using semantic names including a per-product tag like 'product-${handle}' AND a collection tag like 'products'

checklist.name: fetch next.tags used
checklist.description: lib/get-product.ts uses the next: { tags: [...] } option in the fetch call (not a manual cache wrapper)

checklist.name: Targeted invalidation only
checklist.description: The revalidation endpoint does NOT call revalidatePath('/') or any other blanket full-site purge — it only revalidates paths/tags related to the event type

checklist.name: Success response
checklist.description: app/api/revalidate/route.ts returns a JSON response with at least { revalidated: true } on success
